channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Hope your holiday was horrid: You botched the last thing you did before leaving
item.description: The Register's 'Who, Me?' column shares reader confessions about workplace mistakes, highlighting the humorous side of tech-related blunders. This ongoing series provides a light-hearted look at common errors in the tech industry.

item.title: Ask.com, former home of search butler Jeeves, closes just as conversational search comes back
item.description: Ask.com, once a popular search engine featuring the butler Jeeves, has officially closed down as conversational search technology resurfaces. This marks the end of an era for a platform that struggled to adapt to modern search trends.

item.title: Five Eyes spook shops warn agentic is too wonky for rapid rollout
item.description: Five Eyes intelligence agencies advise caution in adopting agentic AI, warning that it may exacerbate existing vulnerabilities within organizations. They emphasize prioritizing resilience over rapid deployment of this technology.

item.title: Just in time for Labour Day, China makes it illegal to fire humans if AI takes their jobs
item.description: A Chinese court has ruled that it is illegal to replace human workers with AI, aiming to protect jobs amid increasing automation. This decision reflects the country's approach to balancing technological advancement with employment concerns.

item.title: I tracked 3,000 steps on my Apple Watch, Google Pixel, and Oura Ring - this one was most accurate
item.description: A comparison of three popular health trackers—Apple Watch, Google Pixel, and Oura Ring—reveals which device most accurately tracked 3,000 steps. The findings provide insights into the reliability of wearable technology.

item.title: This 'cardputer' sits between the Raspberry Pi and Flipper Zero - but it's uniquely better
item.description: The M5Stack Cardputer Adv is introduced as a new pocket computer that bridges the gap between Raspberry Pi and Flipper Zero, offering unique features and portability. This device aims to enhance DIY computing projects.

item.title: burnlens 1.1.0
item.description: The release of burnlens 1.1.0 provides an open-source FinOps proxy for tracking costs associated with OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google Gemini APIs. This tool simplifies financial management for AI projects.

item.title: cyllama-vulkan 0.2.15
item.description: Cyllama-vulkan 0.2.15 is a new Python library designed for local AI inference, utilizing advanced models like llama and whisper. This library aims to streamline AI development without dependencies.

item.title: To our Beloved Brothers in South Africa, by Femi Fani-Kayode
item.description: Femi Fani-Kayode reflects on Shakespeare's 'Julius Caesar,' discussing its themes of ambition and treachery. This piece offers a literary perspective rather than a tech focus.

item.title: cyllama-sycl 0.2.15
item.description: Cyllama-sycl 0.2.15, similar to cyllama-vulkan, is another Python library for local AI inference using state-of-the-art models. This version emphasizes ease of use without dependencies.

item.title: Claude, Microsoft Copilot Fail Again to Predict the Winners of the Kentucky Derby
item.description: AI systems, including Claude and Microsoft Copilot, failed to predict the winners of the Kentucky Derby, continuing a trend of inconsistent performance in such tasks. This highlights the limitations of current AI in making accurate predictions in complex scenarios.

item.title: iPhone 18 series: Check expected price in India, camera upgrades, features and launch timeline
item.description: The upcoming iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max will feature a redesigned display with hidden Face ID sensors and significant camera upgrades, including a mechanical iris. These enhancements aim to improve user experience and photography capabilities.

item.title: copilot-sdk-supercharged 2.1.2
item.description: The GitHub Copilot SDK Supercharged version 2.1.2 has been released, offering programmatic control of the GitHub Copilot CLI and supporting 40 programming languages. This update enhances the usability of Copilot for developers.

item.title: dev-trajectory 0.1.1
item.description: The dev-trajectory 0.1.1 tool allows for local analysis of Claude Code session histories, providing insights into engineering trajectories over time. This can help developers understand their progress and areas for improvement.

item.title: gallama 0.1.5
item.description: Gallama 0.1.5 is introduced as an opinionated Llama Server engine focused on agentic tasks, aiming to streamline specific AI applications. This could enhance the functionality of AI in targeted areas.

item.title: gemcode 0.4.11
item.description: Gemcode 0.4.11 is a local-first coding agent designed for Google Gemini and ADK, enabling developers to work more effectively in their coding environments. This tool emphasizes local development capabilities.

item.title: dev-trajectory 0.1.0
item.description: The dev-trajectory 0.1.0 tool offers local analysis of Claude Code session histories, similar to version 0.1.1, allowing users to visualize their engineering progress over time. This aids in identifying development patterns.

item.title: dev-trajectory added to PyPI
item.description: The dev-trajectory tool has been added to PyPI, making it accessible for developers to analyze their Claude Code session histories. This addition supports the growing ecosystem of developer tools.

item.title: mlx-openai-server 1.8.1
item.description: The mlx-openai-server 1.8.1 is a high-performance API server that provides OpenAI-compatible endpoints for MLX models, enhancing integration capabilities for developers. This update improves the efficiency of using MLX models.

item.title: Events and Announcements: 3 May 2026
item.description: An announcement invites submissions for events and papers to be featured in a future post, focusing on topics like the political afterlives of sexual violence allegations. This highlights ongoing discussions in academia and activism.

item.title: pyg-nightly 2.8.0.dev20260503
item.description: The release of pyg-nightly 2.8.0.dev20260503 introduces updates to the Graph Neural Network Library for PyTorch, enhancing its capabilities for developers. This is significant for those working with machine learning and neural networks.

item.title: gemcli 2.0.4
item.description: GemCLI 2.0.4 is an autonomous AI coding agent that allows users to code via a local web UI and Telegram remote control, providing unlimited access to Gemini. This development signifies a shift towards more accessible AI coding tools.

item.title: langchain-keeperhub 0.5.2
item.description: Langchain-keeperhub 0.5.2 is a toolkit designed for reliable Web3 execution through KeeperHub, enhancing the functionality of decentralized applications. This is important as Web3 continues to grow in relevance.

item.title: nvd-claude-proxy 1.1.0
item.description: The nvd-claude-proxy 1.1.0 allows users to run Claude Code and other Anthropic SDK clients on NVIDIA NIM models via a local proxy. This integration could improve performance and accessibility for developers.

item.title: maidbook added to PyPI
item.description: Maidbook has been added to PyPI as a cache cleaner and health check tool for macOS, aiming to improve system performance. This tool is useful for macOS users looking to maintain their systems efficiently.

item.title: gemcode 0.4.10
item.description: Gemcode 0.4.10 is a local-first coding agent that utilizes Google Gemini and ADK, providing developers with a new way to code efficiently. This reflects the growing trend of local-first applications.

item.title: Public sector banks looks to scale up IT spend in view of cyber threat posed by Anthropic Mythos
item.description: Public sector banks are increasing their IT spending in response to cybersecurity threats posed by Anthropic's Mythos, which has advanced coding capabilities that could exploit system vulnerabilities. This highlights the urgent need for improved cybersecurity in banking.

item.title: Horoscope Tomorrow, 4th May 2026: Love Breakthroughs, Career Growth, Money Signals & Health Guidance
item.description: A horoscope for May 4, 2026, provides predictions on love, career, money, and health for all zodiac signs. While interesting, it doesn't relate to AI or tech developments.

item.title: claude-browse 1.2.2
item.description: Claude-browse 1.2.2 allows users to find and resume past Claude Code sessions from the terminal, featuring an interactive browser with a preview pane. This tool enhances user experience for developers working with Claude Code.

item.title: Public sector banks to ramp up IT spend amid cyber risks from Anthropic's Mythos
item.description: Indian public sector banks are ramping up IT investments to safeguard customer data against potential cyber threats from Anthropic's Mythos. This proactive approach underscores the importance of cybersecurity in financial systems.

item.title: gemcode 0.4.9
item.description: Gemcode 0.4.9 introduces a local-first coding agent that operates on Google Gemini and ADK, enhancing coding efficiency and collaboration. This update signifies a step towards more integrated development environments.

item.title: nn-live 0.1.5
item.description: NN-Live 0.1.5 is a real-time visualizer for neural networks built on PyTorch, allowing developers to monitor and debug models as they run. This tool is significant for improving model transparency and performance.

item.title: jang 2.5.12
item.description: JANG 2.5.12 introduces adaptive mixed-precision quantization for Apple Silicon, enhancing inference capabilities for machine learning models. This update is crucial for optimizing performance on Apple devices.

item.title: llm-observe-proxy 0.2.0
item.description: LLM-Observe-Proxy 0.2.0 is an OpenAI-compatible proxy that captures requests in SQLite and provides an admin UI for better observability. This tool is significant for developers seeking to enhance their AI applications' monitoring.

item.title: Elon Musk put his God complex on trial this week – and proved it is bigger than ever
item.description: Elon Musk's recent courtroom appearance highlighted his self-proclaimed humanitarian mission in AI, but critics argue it showcased his inflated ego. This trial reflects ongoing tensions in the AI industry.

item.title: copilot-sdk-supercharged 2.1.1
item.description: Copilot-SDK-Supercharged 2.1.1 is an upgraded Python SDK for GitHub Copilot, allowing programmatic control across 40 languages. This update enhances the flexibility and usability of Copilot for developers.

item.title: OpenAI employees warned about law enforcement alerts #tech
item.description: OpenAI employees raised alarms about the company's failure to report potential violence described by users to law enforcement, highlighting serious ethical concerns. This situation underscores the importance of responsible AI use.

item.title: NIST CAISI rates DeepSeek V4 Pro lag #tech
item.description: NIST's CAISI benchmark report indicates that DeepSeek V4 Pro is lagging behind U.S. AI models by about eight months, while still being recognized as the most capable Chinese AI model. This highlights competitive dynamics in AI development.

item.title: AI for the Good Guys: The future of truth
item.description: The book 'AI for the Good Guys' discusses two potential futures for AI: one leading to censorship and control, the other promoting transparency and freedom. This perspective is vital for understanding the societal implications of AI.

item.title: Why ChatGPT 5.5 XHigh Mode Might Be the Most Powerful AI You’ve Ever Used
item.description: ChatGPT 5.5's XHigh Mode is gaining attention for its advanced capabilities in handling complex tasks efficiently. This model's balance of power and practicality could redefine user experiences in AI applications.

item.title: I Bought NVIDIA’s $4,699 ‘Personal AI Supercomputer’ … and Then Lost All Optimism For NVIDIA
item.description: The author bought NVIDIA’s expensive AI supercomputer but became disillusioned with its performance and the company's future prospects. This raises concerns about the viability of high-end AI hardware for consumers.

item.title: Show HN: UIGen – Runtime front end for any OpenAPI spec with AI skills
item.description: UIGen is a new tool that allows developers to create front ends for any OpenAPI specification using AI capabilities. It aims to simplify the integration of AI in application development.

item.title: GitHub - yogthos/chiasmus: Chiasmus is an MCP server that gives language models access to formal verification · GitHub
item.description: Chiasmus is a new server that enables language models to access formal verification processes. This could improve the reliability of AI-generated outputs in critical applications.

item.title: featcopilot 0.3.7
item.description: The latest version of featcopilot introduces advanced features for auto feature engineering using GitHub Copilot SDK. This tool aims to enhance productivity in machine learning projects.

item.title: Pat Leahy: Deepfake Simon Harris is only the start of the Government’s AI problem
item.description: Pat Leahy discusses the implications of deepfake technology and AI on politics and employment, highlighting the urgent need for regulatory frameworks. This signals a growing concern over AI's societal impact.

item.title: pyqrack-cuda-complex128 2.0.0
item.description: The release of pyqrack-cuda-complex128 2.0.0 provides a new wrapper for quantum computing simulations in Python. This update enhances the capabilities of quantum programming.

item.title: pyqrack-cuda 2.0.0
item.description: The pyqrack-cuda 2.0.0 release offers a pure Python wrapper for quantum simulations, improving usability for developers in the quantum computing field.

item.title: Alphabet vs Apple Stock 2026: Why GOOGL Edges Out AAPL as the Smarter Buy for Growth Investors
item.description: Investors are comparing Alphabet and Apple stocks, with Alphabet gaining favor due to its AI-driven growth and cloud expansion, suggesting a shift in investment strategies.

item.title: NVIDIA vs Intel Stock Battle 2026: AI Leader or Turnaround Star – Which to Buy Now?
item.description: NVIDIA is favored over Intel in the stock market as analysts predict significant growth for NVIDIA amid the AI boom, while caution is advised for Intel's performance.

item.title: imagine-mcp 1.2.0b6
item.description: The imagine-mcp 1.2.0b6 release introduces a server for image and video understanding and generation, enhancing capabilities for AI applications in media.
